title: ixeris keiskeana
intro: Ixeris keiskeana, also known as Keiske's dandelion, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ixeris keiskeana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exert antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Ixeris keiskeana is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine and Japanese kampo remedies.
